scenario: A team is migrating instances from order-v1 to order-v2. v2 added a new Service Task between validate and ship. Mapping is straightforward for most flow nodes, but the new task has no v1 counterpart. The team wants to know how the migrated instances handle the new task.
question: What happens to migrated instances regarding the new flow node?

options.text: Camunda 8 Desktop Modeler provides built-in BPMN linting through its integrated lint rules engine. As the user edits the diagram, the Modeler highlights elements that violate Camunda 8-specific modelling rules, such as missing task type definitions, unresolved called element references, and improperly configured expression fields. The lint overlay shows inline error and warning markers directly on the canvas, allowing the developer to fix issues before deployment without needing to upload the diagram to a remote cluster.

Camunda 8 Desktop Modeler performs validation only when the user triggers an explicit Schema Validate action from the File menu. 0 XSD schema and reports any structural XML violations. The schema validation does not check Camunda-specific extension attributes or FEEL expression syntax; those checks are performed exclusively by the Zeebe broker at deployment time when the resource is submitted via the gRPC API.

The Desktop Modeler connects to a remote Camunda Validation Service API endpoint to perform all diagram validation. The local application sends the BPMN XML to the cloud validation service, which returns a structured error report in JSON format. Internet connectivity is required for validation, and users working offline must rely on peer review or accept deployment failures as the primary method of discovering modelling errors.

The Desktop Modeler validates BPMN diagrams using a dedicated AI-powered model analysis service that uses machine learning to predict deployment failures. The analysis compares the diagram structure against a corpus of known-good process patterns and assigns a confidence score to each element. Elements with low confidence scores are highlighted in orange, and the user can request an AI-generated fix suggestion by right-clicking on the highlighted element and selecting the Suggest Fix option.

a.text: Correct. Migration applies the new model definition to existing instances at their current execution state. Instances whose token is after the new task position simply continue on v2; instances before that position will encounter the new task when they progress there. No retroactive execution; no auto-skipping.

c.text: Incorrect. Migration handles inserted nodes -- only conflicting mappings might fail.

explanation: Migration applies the new model definition to existing instances at their current execution state. Instances whose token is after the new task position simply continue on v2; instances before that position will encounter the new task when they progress there. No retroactive execution; no auto-skipping.
